league.name: Premier League
league.description: England’s top division: 20 clubs, home-and-away season, three points for a win. It is known for pace, physical duels, and tight races at the top — a good first league to follow if you like constant drama and famous clubs.
league.styleOfPlay: Often end-to-end: quick transitions, pressing, and athletic midfield battles. Top teams mix that with modern positional play; smaller clubs may sit deeper and counter. Less “slow build-up only” than Spain; more sprint and duel intensity.
famousClubs: Manchester City — possession and titles in the 2010s–20s
Arsenal — youth, pressing, north London identity
Liverpool — Anfield atmosphere and European pedigree
Manchester United — global fanbase and historic trophy haul
Chelsea — London club with modern European success
famousPlayers: Erling Haaland — main striker at City
Bukayo Saka — Arsenal winger and England star
Mohamed Salah — Liverpool’s inverted right winger
Kevin De Bruyne — City playmaker and assist king
league.historySummary: The Premier League started in 1992 when top clubs broke away to share TV revenue. It replaced the old First Division branding and grew with global broadcasts. Traditions like Boxing Day fixtures and promotion/relegation keep every match meaningful.
rivalries: North London derby — Arsenal vs Tottenham
Liverpool vs Manchester United — historic national grudge
league.fanGuide: Learn the table: top four often means Champions League, bottom three means relegation. Saturdays remain the rhythm; midweek games pile up in winter. Chants, rivalry weeks, and manager pressure are part of the culture — pick one club and one derby to start.

teams.name: Manchester City
teams.shortHistory: For decades City were the “noisy neighbours” to United. Investment and smart coaching turned them into a Premier League powerhouse, with Pep Guardiola’s teams known for possession, control, and a first Champions League title in 2023.
teams.fanGuide: Sky blue is the colour to know. Supporters sing “Blue Moon” before kickoff at the Etihad. The club’s modern identity is slick football and trophies — less about one legendary era, more about sustained winning since the late 2000s.
legends: Sergio Agüero — 93rd-minute title winner in 2012; City’s greatest goalscorer
David Silva — tiny magician who ran attacks for a decade in sky blue
Vincent Kompany — captain who led the rebuild and scored huge derby goals
Yaya Touré — powerful midfielder; hero of two Premier League titles
currentKeyPlayers: Erling Haaland — centre-forward; main goal threat
Rodri — defensive midfielder; tempo and balance
Phil Foden — local academy star; links midfield and attack
Kevin De Bruyne — creator; crosses and through-balls from the right

teams.name: Arsenal
teams.shortHistory: Arsenal’s 2003–04 “Invincibles” went unbeaten in the league — still a benchmark in English football. After years outside the title race, Mikel Arteta rebuilt with homegrown talent and pressing football, pushing the club back toward the top.
teams.fanGuide: The Gunners wear red and white; the north London derby vs Tottenham is the fixture fans circle first. “North London Forever” plays after big wins at the Emirates. Long-time supporters still talk about the old Highbury ground and the clock on the stadium exterior.
legends: Thierry Henry — club record goalscorer; pace and composure defined the Wenger era
Dennis Bergkamp — non-scoring genius; first touch and link play
Tony Adams — “Mr Arsenal”; leader of the defence for 14 league titles
Patrick Vieira — tall, fierce midfielder; heart of the late 1990s teams
currentKeyPlayers: Bukayo Saka — right wing; goals, assists, and England star
Martin Ødegaard — captain; playmaker between the lines
William Saliba — centre-back; calm on the ball, strong in duels
Declan Rice — defensive midfield; tackles and progression

teams.name: Liverpool
teams.shortHistory: Liverpool built their reputation on European nights at Anfield and a fanbase that travels everywhere. Bill Shankly and Bob Paisley laid the trophy culture; the Klopp years added another Champions League and a first league title in 30 years in 2020.
teams.fanGuide: Red shirts, the Kop end, and “You’ll Never Walk Alone” before kickoff — that song is non-negotiable for newcomers. The Merseyside derby vs Everton is about city pride; matches vs Manchester United carry extra history. European games at Anfield are famous for loud, late comebacks.
legends: Steven Gerrard — one-club captain; Istanbul 2005 and famous long-range goals
Kenny Dalglish — player and manager; symbol of the club in the 1970s–80s
Ian Rush — record goalscorer; poacher in front of goal for years
Jamie Carragher — local defender; 737 appearances, now a vocal pundit
currentKeyPlayers: Mohamed Salah — right wing; cutting inside on his left foot
Virgil van Dijk — centre-back; organises the defensive line
Alisson Becker — goalkeeper; saves and calm distribution
Trent Alexander-Arnold — right-back; set pieces and crossing

teams.name: Chelsea
teams.shortHistory: Chelsea grew from a west London club into a global name through Abramovich-era investment and later American ownership, with repeated Premier League and Champions League success since the 2000s.
teams.fanGuide: Blue is the colour; “Carefree” and Shed End culture matter to supporters. The rivalry with Arsenal and Spurs frames the London football map.
legends: Frank Lampard — midfielder and club-record goals
Didier Drogba — big-game striker
John Terry — captain and defensive leader
currentKeyPlayers: Cole Palmer — attacking midfielder
Enzo Fernández — deep-lying playmaker

teams.name: Manchester United
teams.shortHistory: United built the Busby Babes story, Ferguson’s 26-year dynasty, and a global fanbase. Old Trafford and the 1999 treble remain reference points for English football history.
teams.fanGuide: Red shirts, Stretford End songs, and the Liverpool rivalry define the emotional calendar. “Glory Glory Man United” is the anthem newcomers hear first.
legends: Sir Bobby Charlton — Munich survivor and club icon
George Best — flair winger of the 1960s
Cristiano Ronaldo — two spells, goals and global fame
currentKeyPlayers: Bruno Fernandes — Portugal playmaker and captain
Marcus Rashford — academy forward

teams.name: Tottenham Hotspur
teams.shortHistory: Spurs were the first English club to win a major European trophy in the modern era (1963). The north London rivalry with Arsenal is the club’s heartbeat; recent decades mixed near-miss seasons with new stadium ambition.
teams.fanGuide: White shirts and navy trim; the north London derby is the fixture to learn first. “When the year ends in one, Spurs win something” is a famous supporter joke — know it before you repeat it.
legends: Jimmy Greaves — prolific goalscorer
Glenn Hoddle — elegant playmaker
Harry Kane — academy striker and club record scorer
currentKeyPlayers: Son Heung-min — forward and South Korea captain
James Maddison — creative midfielder

teams.name: Newcastle United
teams.shortHistory: Newcastle carry a passionate regional identity — the Tyne-Wear derby with Sunderland is historic. Modern investment aimed to restore trophy challenges after years of turbulence.
teams.fanGuide: Black and white stripes; Gallowgate End leads the noise. Supporters describe the club as more than football for the north east — learn the Tyne-Wear derby name even if fixtures are less frequent.
legends: Alan Shearer — local hero and Premier League record scorer
Peter Beardsley — technical forward
Kevin Keegan — player and beloved manager
currentKeyPlayers: Alexander Isak — Sweden striker
Bruno Guimarães — Brazil midfielder

teams.name: Aston Villa
teams.shortHistory: Villa are one of England’s founding clubs and European Cup winners in 1982. Birmingham’s claret and blue identity runs deep through the West Midlands.
teams.fanGuide: Claret and blue halves; Holte End is famous for volume. The Second City derby vs Birmingham City is the local grudge to know.
legends: Peter Withe — scorer of the 1982 European Cup final goal
Paul McGrath — elegant defender despite knee issues
Jack Grealish — local academy talent
currentKeyPlayers: Ollie Watkins — England striker
Morgan Rogers — attacking midfielder

teams.name: West Ham United
teams.shortHistory: East London club that has produced generations of England internationals from Geoff Hurst to Declan Rice. Known as the Hammers and famous for their academy's contribution to English football.
teams.fanGuide: West Ham fans are famously passionate. The club moved from Upton Park to the London Stadium in 2016 — the old ground is a touchstone of the identity debate.
legends: Bobby Moore
Geoff Hurst
Martin Peters

teams.name: Brighton & Hove Albion
teams.shortHistory: Brighton rose from the lower leagues to become a data-driven Premier League force under Graham Potter and Roberto De Zerbi. The Amex Stadium opened in 2011 and the club is known for developing and selling elite talent.
teams.fanGuide: The M23 derby against Crystal Palace is the rivalry that matters most. Brighton fans pride themselves on a progressive, modern club culture.

teams.name: Wolverhampton Wanderers
teams.shortHistory: One of the founding members of the Football League, Wolves won three First Division titles in the 1950s. Their modern resurgence under Nuno Espírito Santo brought Portuguese flair and Europa League football.
teams.fanGuide: The Black Country derby against West Brom is fierce. Molineux has one of the most distinctive atmospheres in the Midlands.
legends: Billy Wright
Steve Bull
Robbie Keane

teams.name: Crystal Palace
teams.shortHistory: South London club nicknamed the Eagles with one of the most atmospheric grounds in the Premier League. Known for the passionate Holmesdale Road end and a style built around wide attackers.
teams.fanGuide: The rivalry with Brighton — the M23 derby — defines their season. Selhurst Park's tight stands and vocal support create a unique home advantage.
legends: Ian Wright
Wilfried Zaha
Mark Bright

teams.name: Brentford
teams.shortHistory: West London club that reinvented itself as a data-analytics trailblazer. After 74 years outside the top flight, Brentford won promotion to the Premier League in 2021 and quickly established themselves with intelligent recruitment.
teams.fanGuide: The Bees are a community club that punches above their wage bill. Their approach to player recruitment is studied across the football world.

teams.name: Everton
teams.shortHistory: One of England's most storied clubs, Everton spent the first 130 years of their top-flight life at Goodison Park. Nine league titles and five FA Cups define a proud history. The Merseyside derby against Liverpool is the fiercest local derby in England.
teams.fanGuide: The Merseyside derby is everything. Goodison Park's unique atmosphere and the wall of noise from the bullpen end are unlike anything in English football.
legends: Dixie Dean
Alan Ball
Wayne Rooney

teams.name: Fulham
teams.shortHistory: West London club with one of football's most charming grounds in Craven Cottage, beside the Thames. Fulham are yo-yo club regulars between the Premier League and Championship, known for a technical style and attractive football.
teams.fanGuide: The Cottage's riverside setting is unique in English football. The West Stand's white facade and the neutral Putney End give it a village-football atmosphere even in the top flight.
legends: Johnny Haynes
George Best (briefly)
Clint Dempsey

teams.name: Leeds United
teams.shortHistory: Leeds United is in the FootyBrain controlled expansion club set.
teams.fanGuide: Learn Leeds United's colours and main rivalries as editorial coverage expands.

teams.name: Bournemouth
teams.shortHistory: Bournemouth is in the FootyBrain controlled expansion club set.
teams.fanGuide: Learn Bournemouth's colours and main rivalries as editorial coverage expands.

teams.name: Sunderland
teams.shortHistory: Sunderland is in the FootyBrain controlled expansion club set.
teams.fanGuide: Learn Sunderland's colours and main rivalries as editorial coverage expands.

teams.name: Nottingham Forest
teams.shortHistory: Nottingham Forest is in the FootyBrain controlled expansion club set.
teams.fanGuide: Learn Nottingham Forest's colours and main rivalries as editorial coverage expands.

teams.name: Burnley
teams.shortHistory: Burnley is in the FootyBrain controlled expansion club set.
teams.fanGuide: Learn Burnley's colours and main rivalries as editorial coverage expands.

players.name: Erling Haaland
players.quickFact: His meditation-style goal celebration became one of the Premier League’s most recognizable rituals.
players.playingStyle: Relentless finisher with explosive runs and ruthless positioning in the box.
quizHints: Rose through Molde and Salzburg before a prolific spell in Dortmund yellow.
His father Alf-Inge played in England for Nottingham Forest and Leeds.
Norwegian striker who wears sky blue and often scores with his head.

players.name: Rodri
players.quickFact: Spain fans nicknamed him “El tractor” for how he shields the back line and never stops running.
players.playingStyle: Calm pivot who shields the defense, recycles possession, and occasionally arrives late in the box.
quizHints: Spanish pivot who left Villarreal’s yellow submarine for Atlético briefly.
Scored in Istanbul to help his club complete a treble in 2023.
Often wears number 16 in sky blue — Euro 2024 winner with La Roja.

players.name: Phil Foden
players.quickFact: Wears number 47 at City — a tribute to his grandparents’ house in Moss Side.
players.playingStyle: Silky dribbler who links play between the lines with both feet and clever movement.
quizHints: One-club Premier League attacker raised in Stockport, not Spain.
Pep once compared him to a Barcelona academy legend from the 2000s.
England international in sky blue who plays wide or as a number 10.

players.name: Ederson
players.quickFact: Set up a goal with a kick from his own penalty area in a 2018 Manchester derby — a snapshot of the sweeper-keeper era.
players.playingStyle: Sweeper-keeper with elite distribution and composure under high pressing.
quizHints: Brazilian who won the Premier League Golden Glove in his first season at City.
Left Benfica’s Eagles before joining Guardiola’s possession side.
Often plays long-sleeved with striking tattoos — distribution rivals outfielders.

players.name: Bukayo Saka
players.quickFact: His parents reportedly named him after finding “Bukayo” on a second-hand shopping site.
players.playingStyle: Direct winger with elite ball-striking, cut-inside threat, and tireless defensive work rate.
quizHints: England international who took the decisive spot-kick at Euro 2020’s final.
Hale End graduate who broke through as a left-back before owning the right wing.
Left-footed curls from the right — Arsenal’s number 7 in red and white.

players.name: Martin Ødegaard
players.quickFact: Made his Norway senior debut at 15 — still the youngest debutant in the men’s national team history.
players.playingStyle: Elegant number 10 who orchestrates attacks with disguised passes and clever pressing triggers.
quizHints: Scandinavian playmaker Real Madrid bought as a 16-year-old prodigy.
Loan spells included Heerenveen, Vitesse, and Real Sociedad before north London.
Arsenal captain who wears the armband for Norway — son of a former footballer.

players.name: William Saliba
players.quickFact: Won Ligue 1 Young Player of the Year on loan at Marseille before Arsenal fans finally saw him in red and white.
players.playingStyle: Composed defender who wins duels, carries the ball, and reads through-balls early.
quizHints: French defender Arsenal signed young but waited years to field in the Premier League.
Loan stops included Nice and Marseille before the Emirates partnership chant.
Forms half of a famous centre-back duo with Gabriel — low fade, number 2.

players.name: Declan Rice
players.quickFact: Was West Ham’s youngest-ever captain before a nine-figure move across London in 2023.
players.playingStyle: Box-to-box midfielder with tackling intensity, progressive carrying, and leadership.
quizHints: England midfielder who wore claret and blue before Arsenal’s number 41.
Could have represented Ireland through grandparents but chose England.
Euro 2024 winner who anchors the Gunners’ midfield shield.

players.name: Gabriel Jesus
players.quickFact: His “call to heaven” celebration is a tribute to his mother, who helped raise him in São Paulo.
players.playingStyle: Mobile forward who presses relentlessly, links play, and thrives in wide-to-central rotations.
quizHints: Brazilian forward who won Premier League titles in Manchester before north London.
Palmeiras graduate whose given name matches a biblical figure.
Mimics a phone call after scoring — number 9 in Arsenal red.

players.name: Mohamed Salah
players.quickFact: Worked in a café during a rough patch after Chelsea — before becoming Liverpool’s defining goal scorer of a generation.
players.playingStyle: Left-footed inverted winger who cuts inside for curlers and thrives on counterattacks.
quizHints: African nation whose flag inspires his “Egyptian King” nickname at Anfield.
Left foot on the right wing — the near-post curl is his signature.
Roma’s “Pharaoh” before Merseyside records and Premier League Golden Boots.

players.name: Virgil van Dijk
players.quickFact: PFA Player of the Year in 2019 — one of the few defenders to win England’s top individual prize.
players.playingStyle: Dominant aerial defender with calm distribution and game-reading that organizes the back line.
quizHints: Dutch defender who wore Celtic hoops and Southampton red-and-white before Anfield.
Southampton sold him for a then-record fee for a defender in 2018.
Wears number 4, leads Liverpool’s high line — recovered from a serious knee injury.

players.name: Alisson Becker
players.quickFact: Scored a 95th-minute header from a corner in May 2021 — one of only a handful of Premier League goals by a keeper.
players.playingStyle: Shot-stopper with quick reflexes and calm one-on-one defending in big moments.
quizHints: Brazilian shot-stopper whose brother also keeps professionally in Portugal.
Roma’s number one before Liverpool made him the world’s most expensive keeper at the time.
Famous late winning header against a Midlands club in green and white stripes.

players.name: Trent Alexander-Arnold
players.quickFact: Wears number 66 — a nod to the year his postal district was founded, not a random squad number.
players.playingStyle: Quarterback full-back who delivers whipped crosses and set-piece mastery over defensive orthodoxy.
quizHints: Scouse academy graduate from West Derby who never left his boyhood club.
Among the Premier League’s most prolific assist-makers for a defender.
Set-piece delivery from the right touchline — free-kicks and corners for Liverpool.

players.name: Darwin Núñez
players.quickFact: Almería bought him from Uruguay for about €4M and sold him to Benfica for roughly eight times that one season later.
players.playingStyle: Chaotic runner who presses high, attacks channels, and finishes with power over finesse.
quizHints: Uruguayan striker whose hair colour changes as often as debate about his finishing.
Portuguese league top scorer season preceded a big Anfield transfer fee.
Often plays central in a front three beside Egypt’s king on the right wing.

players.name: Christian Nørgaard
players.quickFact: Denmark midfielder who captained Brentford before Arsenal signed him as a Premier League anchor with aerial strength and tackling.
players.playingStyle: Physical holding midfielder who breaks up play, wins headers, and keeps possession moving with simple passes.
quizHints: Denmark international defensive midfielder at Arsenal.
Tall pivot known for tackling, aerial duels, and shielding the back line.
Midfielder whose first name is Christian and surname is Nørgaard.

players.name: Eberechi Eze
players.quickFact: England playmaker whose Crystal Palace brilliance earned a big Arsenal move and a place in the Euro 2024 squad.
players.playingStyle: Silky attacking midfielder who dribbles inside, shoots from distance, and unlocks defences with through-balls.
quizHints: England attacking midfielder who went to Euro 2024.
Arsenal in red after starring for Crystal Palace in south London.
Eberechi Eze — left-footed creator, not Bukayo Saka the winger.

players.name: Jurriën Timber
players.quickFact: Netherlands defender who won the Eredivisie with Ajax before Arsenal made him a versatile right-back in the Premier League.
players.playingStyle: Composed defender who defends tightly, steps into midfield, and distributes accurately under pressure.
quizHints: Netherlands international defender who came through Ajax’s academy.
Can play right-back or centre-back with strong one-on-one defending.
Defender whose first name is Jurriën and surname is Timber.

players.name: Mikel Merino
players.quickFact: Spain midfielder who drove La Roja’s Euro 2024 triumph and joined Arsenal after years as a Real Sociedad mainstay.
players.playingStyle: Box-to-box midfielder who presses intensely, wins aerial duels, and arrives late in the penalty area.
quizHints: Spain midfielder who won Euro 2024 in Germany.
Former Real Sociedad captain before Arsenal in red and white.
Mikel Merino — not Mikel from other nations; box-to-box energy.

players.name: Noni Madueke
players.quickFact: England winger whose dribbling at PSV and Chelsea earned him an Arsenal move as a direct Premier League wide threat.
players.playingStyle: Skillful winger who beats defenders one-on-one, attacks the byline, and finishes with power from wide areas.
quizHints: England international winger who developed at PSV Eindhoven.
Direct wide player known for dribbling and cutting inside to shoot.
Attacker whose first name is Noni and surname is Madueke.

players.name: Piero Hincapié
players.quickFact: Ecuador centre-back in the linked La Tri squad who developed at Bayer Leverkusen before Arsenal in the Premier League.
players.playingStyle: Left-sided centre-back who carries forward, tackles aggressively, and builds from the back.
quizHints: Ecuadorian centre-back linked to La Tri.
Arsenal defender — not Moisés Caicedo the midfielder.
Piero who steps out from the back and wins aerial duels for Ecuador.

players.name: Amadou Onana
players.quickFact: Belgium defensive midfielder whose size and ball-winning grew at Everton before Aston Villa made him a Premier League anchor.
players.playingStyle: Athletic holding midfielder who shields the back line, wins duels, and progresses play with powerful strides.
quizHints: Belgium international defensive midfielder with Premier League experience.
Tall pivot known for interceptions, aerial duels, and carrying the ball forward.
Midfielder whose first name is Amadou and surname is Onana.

players.name: Emiliano Buendía
players.quickFact: Argentina attacking midfielder whose Norwich brilliance earned a Premier League move to Aston Villa and senior Albiceleste caps.
players.playingStyle: Technical attacking midfielder who drifts inside from the left, combines in tight spaces, and slips through-balls.
quizHints: Argentina no. 10-style creator at Aston Villa in claret and blue.
Breakout with Norwich in the Championship before the Premier League.
Emiliano with the accent — not Emi Martínez the goalkeeper.

players.name: Emiliano Martínez
players.quickFact: Argentina goalkeeper whose penalty saves and personality became part of the 2022 World Cup story.
players.playingStyle: Commanding goalkeeper who thrives in one-on-ones, penalties, and emotional high-pressure moments.
quizHints: Spent years at Arsenal before becoming a Premier League number one elsewhere.
World Cup winner famous for penalty shootout confidence.
Argentina keeper who plays for Aston Villa.

players.name: Ian Maatsen
players.quickFact: Netherlands left-back whose attacking overlap at Chelsea and on loan led to Aston Villa as a Premier League wide defender.
players.playingStyle: Quick left-back who pushes high, delivers crosses, and recovers with speed to defend one-on-ones.
quizHints: Netherlands international left-back with Chelsea academy roots.
Attacking full-back known for pace and overlapping runs.
Defender whose first name is Ian and surname is Maatsen.

players.name: John McGinn
players.quickFact: Scotland international midfielder in the linked Tartan Army national pool.
players.playingStyle: Energetic central midfielder who drives forward and shoots from distance.
quizHints: Scottish midfielder linked to Scotland.
Central midfielder — not Gilmour the younger no. 6.
Plays in the Premier League.

players.name: Morgan Rogers
players.quickFact: England attacking midfielder whose dribbling and goal contributions helped Aston Villa push for Champions League places.
players.playingStyle: Direct ball-carrier who drives at defences, combines in transition, and arrives late with powerful shooting.
quizHints: England youth international who developed at Manchester City before Villa.
Tall ball-carrier who can play wide or through the middle.
Aston Villa attacker whose first name is Morgan and surname is Rogers.

players.name: Ollie Watkins
players.quickFact: England striker who scored prolifically for Brentford and Aston Villa with relentless pressing and penalty-box movement.
players.playingStyle: Energetic number nine who presses defenders, attacks channels, and finishes with sharp movement in the box.
quizHints: England international centre-forward who came through Exeter and Brentford.
Forward known for high work rate and timing of runs in behind.
Striker whose first name is Ollie and surname is Watkins.

players.name: Youri Tielemans
players.quickFact: Belgium midfielder whose passing range and composure featured for Leicester before Aston Villa made him a Premier League playmaker.
players.playingStyle: Composed midfielder who dictates tempo, switches play, and threatens from outside the box with clean striking technique.
quizHints: Belgium international midfielder known for long passing and vision.
Scored a famous FA Cup final goal for Leicester from distance.
Aston Villa midfielder whose first name is Youri and surname is Tielemans.

players.name: Aaron Hickey
players.quickFact: Scotland right-back who broke through at Bologna before Premier League spells with Brentford.
players.playingStyle: Athletic full-back who overlaps on the right, defends one-on-ones, and supports attacks with steady crossing.
quizHints: Scotland international full-back from the Edinburgh area.
Played in Italy with Bologna before moving to England.
Brentford right-back whose first name is Aaron and surname is Hickey.

players.name: Dango Ouattara
players.quickFact: Burkina Faso winger whose pace and direct running made him a Brentford standout after arriving from Lorient.
players.playingStyle: Explosive wide player who drives at defenders, cuts inside, and creates chaos with pace and aggressive pressing.
quizHints: Burkina Faso international who attacks primarily from wide areas.
Moved from French football to Brentford in the Premier League.
Brentford winger whose first name is Dango and plays with high tempo.

players.name: Igor Thiago
players.quickFact: Brazil striker whose physical finishing and movement in the box made him a Brentford goal threat after developing in Belgium.
players.playingStyle: Powerful number nine who contests aerial duels, pins defenders, and finishes with force inside the box.
quizHints: Brazil centre-forward known for strength and penalty-box positioning.
Tall striker who attacks crosses and holds up play with his back to goal.
Forward whose first name is Igor and surname is Thiago.

players.name: Michael Kayode
players.quickFact: Italy right-back whose pace and attacking runs at Brentford made him a modern overlapping full-back in the Premier League.
players.playingStyle: Athletic right-back who flies forward, delivers crosses, and recovers with speed to defend counters.
quizHints: Italy youth international right-back at Brentford in the Premier League.
Fast full-back known for recovery runs and attacking the byline.
Defender whose first name is Michael and surname is Kayode.

players.name: Yegor Yarmolyuk
players.quickFact: Ukraine midfielder whose creativity at Brentford made him a Premier League playmaker with dribbling and vision from central areas.
players.playingStyle: Skillful midfielder who carries the ball forward, combines in tight spaces, and unlocks defences with incisive passes.
quizHints: Ukraine youth international midfielder at Brentford.
Technical central player known for dribbling and passing between lines.
Midfielder whose first name is Yegor and surname is Yarmolyuk.

players.name: Adam Webster
players.quickFact: England centre-back who became a Brighton defensive leader after spells at Ipswich and Bristol City.
players.playingStyle: Composed centre-back who steps into midfield passes, organizes the line, and defends firmly in the air.
quizHints: England defender who helped Brighton establish as a Premier League regular.
Centre-back known for calm passing and aerial defending.
Brighton defender whose first name is Adam and surname is Webster.

players.name: Bart Verbruggen
players.quickFact: Netherlands international goalkeeper in the linked Oranje national pool.
players.playingStyle: Modern goalkeeper who sweeps behind the line and plays out from the back.
quizHints: Dutch goalkeeper linked to the Netherlands.
Orange kit nation — keeper, not Frimpong the full-back.
Plays in the Premier League.

players.name: Danny Welbeck
players.quickFact: England striker whose career path ran through Manchester United and Arsenal before becoming a Brighton veteran.
players.playingStyle: Mobile striker who links play, presses from the front, and finishes with clever movement rather than pure power.
quizHints: Scored a famous FA Cup final goal for Manchester United as a teenager.
Played for England at a home European Championship.
Experienced centre-forward at Brighton wearing Seagulls blue and white.

players.name: Ferdi Kadıoğlu
players.quickFact: Turkey left-back who broke through at Fenerbahçe before Brighton and offers overlapping width for the national team.
players.playingStyle: Modern full-back who overlaps, inverts when needed, and defends with quick recovery runs.
quizHints: Turkey left-back in red and white who plays for Brighton.
Fenerbahçe graduate — not Zeki Çelik on the right.
Ferdi who attacks the left channel with pace.

players.name: Igor Julio
players.quickFact: Brazil left-back who rebuilt his Premier League reputation at Brighton with calm defending and left-footed build-up play.
players.playingStyle: Composed left-back who defends the flank with timing, supports attacks with underlaps, and circulates possession cleanly.
quizHints: Brazil international left-back who played in France before England.
Left-footed defender comfortable defending one-on-one and passing out from the back.
Defender whose first name is Igor and surname is Julio.

players.name: Mats Wieffer
players.quickFact: Netherlands defensive midfielder whose screening and passing became central to Brighton’s midfield after Eredivisie success.
players.playingStyle: Disciplined holding midfielder who intercepts, receives under pressure, and distributes with consistent tempo.
quizHints: Netherlands international holding midfielder with Eredivisie roots.
Calm recycler who breaks up play and keeps possession moving.
Defensive midfielder whose first name is Mats and surname is Wieffer.

players.name: Yasin Ayari
players.quickFact: Sweden midfielder whose energy and ball-winning at Brighton made him a Premier League box-to-box runner after arriving from Belgium.
players.playingStyle: Dynamic midfielder who covers ground, wins second balls, and links defence to attack with direct carries.
quizHints: Sweden international central midfielder at Brighton in the Premier League.
Energetic midfielder known for pressing, tackling, and carrying the ball forward.
Midfielder whose first name is Yasin and surname is Ayari.

players.name: Andrey Santos
players.quickFact: Brazil midfielder who came through Vasco da Gama and Chelsea’s system as a powerful ball-carrier and goal threat from midfield.
players.playingStyle: Strong midfielder who drives forward, shoots from distance, and combines physicality with technical finishing.

players.name: Yerson Mosquera
